title: Athena's Warning
tagline: She's finished the scroll. All seven sections. She knows what's coming. Now she has to tell everyone — and figure out how.
arc_note: PRE-FINALE SETUP — Athena has completed her research. She knows the Titan will reach the surface. She knows it will be during the next full moon (three days away). She calls the group together and tells them everything she knows. This is the episode where the season's scattered threads converge: Hercules's engineering knowledge, Zeus's precision, Perseus's connection to Medusa, Achilles's coaching, the Olympus Sea (Poseidon's gift, relevant now).

scenes.title: The Full Picture
scenes.narration: Athena had been up for thirty-six hours. Not because she was afraid — because she had found the final piece. Cross-referencing her father's old records (which she had technically borrowed without asking, but she would address that later) with the tidal data and the geological surveys: the Titan below Olympus would reach the surface at the next full moon. Three days. She closed the scroll. She opened a fresh one. She wrote: WE NEED TO TALK.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Athena at her desk, surrounded by open scrolls, thirty-six hours deep. Finding the final piece — cross-reference click. The tidal data and geological survey aligning. Full moon: three days. She closes one scroll. Opens a fresh one. Writes WE NEED TO TALK in large letters. Archie watches her. He has been awake the whole time too. 16:9.

scenes.title: She Tells Them Everything
scenes.narration: She gathered everyone. She told them everything: the Titan below Olympus, what it was, how long it had been there, what the full moon meant. She showed them the full scroll — all seven sections. She didn't soften it. She said: 'We have three days. We don't have to fight it — Titans can be redirected. But the redirection requires specific things from each of us.' She listed them. As she listed, she looked at each of them. They had been preparing for this without knowing.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Full group assembled, Athena standing with the open scroll. She tells them everything — no softening. Their reactions: Zeus focused (he knew something was coming), Hercules nodding with surprising gravity (he has the engineering knowledge), Perseus quiet and thinking, Achilles still. She lists what each needs to do. Each realization: they've been preparing. 16:9.

scenes.title: What Each of Them Has
scenes.narration: Zeus: precision thunderbolts to seal the east crack (two weeks of practice). Hercules: structural engineering knowledge to reinforce the key pressure points (absorbed from Athena's scrolls, now confirmed on his own). Perseus: Medusa. Medusa's stone-turning ability, precisely applied, could seal the surface cracks permanently — but only if she agreed to help. Achilles: he would run the coordination, position everyone, call the timing — the coaching role he'd discovered in EP005. Athena: she had the plan.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Athena's assignment list — each person and their specific role shown as she names them. Zeus with his thunderbolt stance. Hercules with his engineering scrolls. Perseus with his shield (thinking about Medusa). Achilles in ready position. Athena with the master scroll. The plan becoming visible. 16:9.

scenes.title: But Medusa
scenes.narration: Perseus said: 'I can ask her. But I can't make her say yes.' Athena said: 'I know.' He said: 'She might say no.' Athena said: 'That's a risk we take. All we can do is ask clearly, honestly, and let her choose.' He said: 'What if she says no and we need her?' Athena said: 'Then we find another way. But we ask first.' Perseus nodded. He already knew he was going to ask. He'd been thinking about it since she said 'Medusa.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Perseus and Athena — their quiet exchange about Medusa. His concern about forcing her. Athena's answer: ask honestly, let her choose. His nod. He was going to ask before she finished the sentence. 16:9.

scenes.title: One More Thing
scenes.narration: Athena said: 'One more thing. The Titan will be drawn toward the Olympus Sea — water, light, open space. Uncle Poseidon made something very useful without knowing it.' She looked at the Olympus Sea visible from the window. 'If the Titan moves toward the sea instead of the mountain, we have more time and a better position. Poseidon needs to know.' Zeus said: 'I'll write to him tonight.' He didn't add: I'll check the address. He didn't need to be told.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Athena gesturing to the Olympus Sea — Poseidon's accidental gift, now strategically crucial. Zeus already composing the letter in his head. He doesn't need the reminder about addresses. The Olympus Sea sparkling in the background. All the season's threads visible in one window. 16:9.

scenes.title: Three Days
scenes.narration: Three days of preparation. Zeus practiced the specific crack-sealing shot. Hercules reinforced pressure points from his engineering scrolls. Perseus went to Medusa's valley — we don't hear what was said, but he came back that evening with a nod. Achilles planned the coordination sequence, timed to the second. Poseidon's reply arrived: he understood, he'd be ready, he brought four extra dolphins who were very motivated. Athena updated the scroll.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. THREE-DAY MONTAGE — Zeus at practice, Hercules at pressure points, Perseus going to the valley and returning with a nod, Achilles with his coordination tablet, Poseidon's enthusiastic reply scroll (with dolphin illustrations in the margin). Athena's scroll gaining its final entries. 16:9.

scenes.title: What Did We Learn?
scenes.narration: A good plan uses what everyone already has. Not what you wish they had — what they actually have, from who they actually are. Athena built a plan from real materials: Zeus's practice, Hercules's absorbed knowledge, Perseus's relationship with someone unexpected, Achilles's coaching, an uncle's accidental sea. All real. All earned. See you next time — we're almost there.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Final card — the group together in pre-finale energy, each holding their role: Zeus with thunderbolt, Hercules with scrolls, Perseus with polished shield, Achilles with his coordination notes, Athena with the master scroll. Ready. The mountain behind them. Full moon in two days. 16:9.

lesson: The best plan uses what everyone actually has — real skills, real relationships, real preparation. Not what you wish you had.
youtube_title: Athena Reveals EVERYTHING She Knows About the Titan! 📜 | Little Olympus Episode 18
next_episode_preview: Episode 19 — The Night Before! Full moon tomorrow. Everyone is ready. Almost. Achilles's heel is aching again.
